card.intuition: A messaging pattern where a message sent by one 'Publisher' is received by all 'Subscribers' who are interested in that specific topic.
card.analogy: A magazine subscription: when the latest issue of 'Backender' is published, every single person who subscribed gets their own copy of the same magazine.
card.time_complexity: O(N) where N is number of subscribers
when_to_use: Updating multiple microservices at once when a piece of data changes
Real-time features like chat rooms where everyone in the room gets the message
Decoupling the sender from an unknown number of receivers
gotchas: If there are NO subscribers listening when a message is published, the message is often lost (unless the broker has 'Durable' subscriptions)
It can lead to 'Message Storms' if one event triggers hundreds of fan-out actions at once

steps.label: Publisher
steps.annotation: Publisher emits event to the event bus — no knowledge of subscribers.

steps.label: Event Bus
steps.annotation: Bus routes message to all subscribers of that topic.

steps.label: Subscriber B
steps.annotation: Subscriber B processes independently — fully decoupled.

steps.label: Decouple
steps.annotation: New subscribers added without changing publisher code.

exercise.prompt: Identify the roles in a Pub/Sub system.
exercise.starter_code:
roles = [
    "The source of the news: The _________",
    "The interested reader: The _________",
    "The news category: The _____"
]
exercise.test_code:
assert "Publisher" in roles[0]
assert "Subscriber" in roles[1]
assert "Topic" in roles[2]
print("PASS")
